name: Parapet (roof-edge battlement)
description: A low decorative wall along the top edge of a building, sitting one course above the footprint AABB. Composed of an optional 1-block-tall cornice ring (@slab for medieval/fantasy, @accent for modern) one course below the parapet, the parapet ring itself (1-block-tall @primary along the perimeter), optional crenellations (alternating merlons + crenels every 2 blocks for medieval/fantasy/gothic/viking — modern stays smooth), and optional corner posts (single @accent blocks at each of the 4 corners, sticking 1 block above the parapet). Differs from `perimeter_wall_fortified`: the parapet is a thin decorative rim, not a thick defensive curtain. Works on any footprint from 3×1×3 to 30×3×30; nothing is emitted inside the footprint — only along the perimeter ring.

required_furniture.rule: 1-block-tall ring of @primary along the perimeter at y_top (one course above the AABB).

When `crenellated` is on (default for medieval/fantasy/gothic/viking), the parapet ring is patterned every 2 blocks — odd-indexed cells around the ring are cleared to air, leaving alternating merlons (block) and crenels (gap).
Optional 1-block-tall ring at y_top - 1 just below the parapet. Uses @slab on medieval / fantasy (a moulding) and @accent on modern (a flat band). Toggle with `cornice` kwarg (default True).

Four single @accent blocks, one at each corner of the perimeter, placed 1 block ABOVE the parapet ring (y_top + 1). The parapet cell directly under each post is re-stamped @primary so the post never lands on a crenel gap. Toggle with `corner_posts` kwarg (default True).

placement_rules: Parapet ring sits one course above the input footprint AABB (at y1).
Cornice course (if enabled) sits one course below the parapet ring (at y1 - 1).
Corner posts stick 1 block above the parapet ring (at y1 + 1).
Only the perimeter is touched — nothing is emitted inside the footprint.
Modern style defaults to a smooth (non-crenellated) parapet; medieval / fantasy / gothic / viking default to crenellated.
lighting_ventilation: The parapet is an exterior decorative element with no interior; ventilation is not applicable. On crenellated variants, the crenels (gaps between merlons) provide visual openings every 2 blocks around the perimeter, evoking sightlines from a wall-walk below.

medieval.notes: Crenellated cobblestone battlement with stone-brick corner posts and a cobblestone-slab cornice — classic castle silhouette.

modern.notes: Smooth concrete parapet (no crenellations) with smooth-quartz corner accents and a smooth-stone-slab moulding band — a minimalist roof-edge crown.

fantasy.notes: Crenellated purpur parapet with glowing sea-lantern corner posts and a purpur-slab cornice — evokes an Elven or End-themed citadel.

python_skill_signature: pipeline.skills.parapet.build(aabb, materials, style, *, crenellated=None, cornice=True, corner_posts=True) -> list[Op]
style_affinity: Medieval / fortified-military motif. Coherent in medieval/castle/rustic/fantasy/gothic. Out of place in Modern, Minimalist, East-Asian, Classical/Renaissance buildings.
